subject: Custom Query Code
commit_message:
Custom Query Code

Changes to aai, so, vfc and restmanager to support aai custom queries.
updated to latest version of aai schema. Made changes according to
latest updates.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1278
Change-Id: I255cef17fff4fe7d4ea21344c0e5ccaac52cbe9a
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>

Patch Set 4:

(6 comments)

some comments on the AAI API Versioning

Patch Set 4:

> (6 comments)
 > 
 > some comments on the AAI API Versioning
Thanks for the comments. In my local setup I can't import v16 versions (I can do it only till v15). I am using the dependency below. Please advise. 
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.onap.aai.schema-service</groupId>
            <artifactId>aai-schema</artifactId>
            <version>1.0.1</version>
        </dependency>

Patch Set 4:

> (6 comments)
 > 
 > some comments on the AAI API Versioning

Also, I am not sure how to avoid hardcoding of APIs. Are you suggesting to simply make it a static variable or is there a way to get them dynamically?

Patch Set 4:

> @pramod unfortunately you are correct. Please raise a bug on AAI
 > schema-service.
 > The schema files for v16 (OXM, XSD, etc) are in that 1.0.1 release
 > artifact, but the yang classes for v16 are somehow missing.

I was unable to find the exact Issue-ID for Aai Schema-service, can you tell me the number.

(1 comment)

> Also, I am not sure how to avoid hardcoding of APIs. Are you
 > suggesting to simply make it a static variable or is there a way to
 > get them dynamically?

I guess with this code structure, there is no way to avoid importing a specific API version. Maybe the only hope is to wrap it so that it only needs to be maintained in one place, rather than several files.

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > > Also, I am not sure how to avoid hardcoding of APIs. Are you
 > > suggesting to simply make it a static variable or is there a way
 > to
 > > get them dynamically?
 > 
 > I guess with this code structure, there is no way to avoid
 > importing a specific API version. Maybe the only hope is to wrap it
 > so that it only needs to be maintained in one place, rather than
 > several files.

@Keong - Thanks for the comments. Harish said the latest A&AI schema version is always in org.onap.aai.domain.yang. I have made the corresponding changes in the latest patch. Regarding setting the version via a config, I looked at the other (old) code which accesses named queries (they are also kind of set in the code) and I think such  feature does not exist as of now.

Patch Set 6:

@Pam None of my code here /policy/models conflicts with what Vamshi has submitted. So I think I am okay if this is merged.

Ok no problem - please keep me up-to-date.

Patch Set 6:

Pramod, one question, and one request: 
1) are changes needed in the simulators packages?   
2) can you clone the latest drools-application and do "mvn clean install" after you have built this one, to make sure if there are any side effects in that repo?
Thank you!

Patch Set 8:

Hi,
I have pulled in the latest git, and I can successfully build /policy/models on my local machine. When I look at the logs, it shows an error in models/policy-models-pdp/ModelsTest.java: Line 44. I never modified that sub-project or file. So I am not sure why it fails the build by ONAPJobBuilder (when the whole models project successfully builds on my local machine). Any suggestions?
